scenario: Live Codex control, desktop mutation, and public closeout comment recommendations must fail closed unless the request has matching proof, approval, and public-safe hygiene evidence.
expected_public_safe_evidence: blocker code for missing or mismatched approval
visible-map actionsPerformed flags proving no live control, GUI mutation, or raw transcript read
fallback-status actionsPerformed flags proving no live control, GUI mutation, screenshot capture, refresh, restart, click, select, or type
action-bound Desktop collaboration proof blockers for generic GUI action, live Codex control, missing approval, mismatched hash, stale approval, and source coverage gaps
tool command-policy mutationClasses distinguishing pure reads from derived_cache, source_store, external_system, live_control, desktop_gui, release_publish, and npm_publish families
public comment hygiene blocker codes and redacted previews for malformed closeout bodies
persisted watcher events and local attention queue items with opaque refs, source coverage, execute=false tool calls, mutates=false observations, and no approval ids minted
prepared-state card and inbox outputs with opaque refs, source coverage, authority coverage, stale/partial/unknown states, execute=false actions, and no raw transcript/path payloads

hook sidecar capture outputs with hook_capture_packets refs, state_prep_jobs refs, transcriptPathHash, transcriptPathRedacted true, summaryCaptured false, trueCompactionSummaryCaptured false, and false action flags for Codex mutation, source-store mutation, external writes, live control, GUI mutation, raw transcript reads, and model compaction
pass_criteria: Live send, steer, resume, and interrupt fail without a matching approval audit id.
Message-bearing dry runs include params_hash and message_hash without storing raw prompts.
Desktop action remains dry-run-only unless a future backend-specific approval gate is proven.
Audit output is redacted and source-ref based.
Codex app-server read tools expose status/list/map only and never call includeTurns:true or mutation methods.
Visible map ambiguity is reported as low confidence instead of authorizing a select, focus, continue, or GUI fallback action.
Codex Desktop fallback status reports CUA/Peekaboo blockers and warnings without performing or authorizing the fallback action.
Codex Desktop collaboration proof fails closed unless the target ref, backend, target app/window, action, action hash, approval packet, freshness, source coverage, and no-screenshot policy all match.

Indexing, watcher persistence, and dry-run audit tools are labeled derived_cache only; watcher request and watcher event read tools remain pure reads, and live/GUI/release/external mutation classes stay out of default prepared-state paths.

Summary leaf list and expansion tools remain pure reads over metadata-only derived cache, while any summary-leaf materialization is classified as derived_cache and omits raw transcript spans and raw tool payloads.
Prepared card and inbox read tools remain pure reads over public-safe derived cache, while materialization is classified as derived_cache and next actions are advisory `execute:false` packets only.

Hook closeout capture, state prep, and compaction marker commands are classified as derived_cache writes only; transcript paths are hash/redact-only, state-prep packets come from existing prepared-state reports, and marker mode cannot capture or claim a true compaction summary.
Closeout dry-run blocks wouldAttach when the proposed public comment body repeats local path fragments or becomes path-token dense.
fail_criteria: Any live action can run without prior dry-run proof.
Any approval proof accepts a different target ref, action, params hash, or message hash.
Evidence stores raw prompt text, raw transcript spans, screenshots, SQLite pages, or secrets.
A tool mutates LCO-owned cache or audit state while declaring no mutation class, or a watcher/prepared-state read implies live control or external mutation.
Watcher event reads or local attention queue items mint approval ids, set execute=true, expose raw transcript paths or prompt spans, or suggest live-control/GUI tools as executable actions.
Summary leaf outputs expose raw prompts, raw tool-call arguments, absolute transcript paths, or hidden model-compaction claims instead of metadata-only leaf refs and omission markers.
Prepared card or inbox outputs expose raw transcript paths, raw prompt spans, raw tool payloads, secrets, high-confidence stale cache, or executable live-control/GUI actions.

Hook sidecar commands expose raw transcript paths, raw hook message text, secrets, live-control/GUI executable actions, model-compaction output, source-store mutation, external writes, or a true compaction-summary capture claim.
Read-only app-server tools expose preview, cwd, path, turns, or remote-control enablement fields as public evidence.
Public GitHub issue or PR comment recommendations include repeated absolute local paths or raw evidence-root spam instead of concise public-safe summaries.
known_gaps: Approved harmless live-control smoke still requires explicit user approval before it can become proof.
Desktop fallback status and Codex Desktop collaboration proof are read-only/dry-run; Desktop fallback mutation remains outside proof unless a future exact action-bound observation passes.
Visible Codex map joins are read-only correlation proof only, not a Codex GUI control proof.
Hook sidecar compaction capture is marker-only. True Codex compaction-summary capture still requires Codex-native sanitized event support or a separately proven adapter.
next_action: Keep live control, GUI mutation, and public comment posting excluded from automated paths unless a future issue records explicit approved runtime proof and public-comment hygiene pass evidence.
proof_boundary: Scored as public-safe beta safety evidence for current fail-closed control, read-only app-server, visible-map, desktop coherence, desktop fallback status, desktop proof-action, action-bound dry-run Codex Desktop collaboration proof, prepared cards/inbox over derived cache, persisted watcher events with execute-false local attention queue items, hook sidecar closeout/state-prep/marker-only compaction capture through issue #412, and public closeout comment hygiene tests. It does not prove live Codex control, GUI mutation, Codex GUI mutation, generic desktop control, unattended operation, model compaction, true Codex compaction-summary capture, source-store mutation, external writes, or automated GitHub comment posting.
